UI.NoCompendiumSelectedDesc: Please select a compendium pack from the left sidebar to browse and add its contents.

AvailabilityRule.name: Availability Rules
AvailabilityRule.hint: Selection options for the Rules you want to use, standard is RAW

ApprovalWorkflow.name: GM Approval Workflow
ApprovalWorkflow.hint: Toggle this setting to Disable the approval workflow for player purchases!

ChatRequestEnabled.name: Chat Message: Purchase Requests
ChatRequestEnabled.hint: Post a chat message when a purchase request is submitted for review.

ChatRejectionEnabled.name: Chat Message: Rejections
ChatRejectionEnabled.hint: Post a chat message when a request or an item is rejected.

ChatApprovalEnabled.name: Chat Message: Approvals
ChatApprovalEnabled.hint: Post a chat message when a purchase is approved.

SendToSmartphone.name: Send Messages to Smartphone Widget
SendToSmartphone.hint: Route order confirmation and rejection messages directly to the player's smartphone messages app instead of posting them to the chat log.

ResetItemLoad.name: Reset Item Enhancement Load
ResetItemLoad.hint: This will allow the karma values and cost/availability of world and compendium items to get their flags reinitialized. Setting this setting to true will undo any changes you made to Karma, cost, or Availability of the items.

PremiumThemes.name: Enable Premium Themes
PremiumThemes.hint: Make premium custom themes (Apple Silicon, Cyber Neon, Neon Light) pickable in the core Foundry UI configuration menu.

Settings.title: Marketplace Settings
Settings.description: Click on an item type in any column to assign it to that behavior category. Changes are saved instantly. Unique items can only be purchased once per character, stacking items will be stacked in the same item, and single items can be ordered multiple times per order as one Item Instance.

Menu.name: Item Type Menu
Menu.hint: This menu allows you to assign item types to different behaviors.

KarmaSpell.name: Karma Spell Cost
KarmaSpell.hint: This setting allows you to set the karma cost for spells. Value will be used to pay for spells.

KarmaComplexForm.name: Karma Complex Form Cost
KarmaComplexForm.hint: This setting allows you to set the karma cost for complex forms. Value will be used to pay for Complex Forms.

owner.hint: The UUID of the Actor who owns this shop.

employees.label: Employees
employees.hint: A list of Actor UUIDs who are employees of this shop.

connection.label: Connection
connection.hint: The UUID of an Item that represents the shop's primary Connection.

modifierValue.label: Modifier Value
modifierValue.hint: The numeric value of the fee or discount.

modifierType.label: Modifier Type
modifierType.hint: Whether the modifier is a fee or a discount.

GettingStarted.Title: Getting Started with SR5 Marketplace
GettingStarted.Description: Learn how to create a shop, link connections, place it on the canvas, and run player purchases and GM approvals.

Intro.Title: Welcome to SR5 Marketplace!
Intro.Content: This step-by-step tour will guide you through setting up a shop actor, populating it with wares, linking it to a player contact connection and employee, placing it on the scene canvas, and executing buying & GM approval workflows.

CreateActor.Title: Create a Shop Actor
CreateActor.Content: Start by opening the Actors Directory tab in the sidebar. Click the Create Actor button. In the creation dialog, make sure to choose the Shop actor type and name your shop.

PopulateShop.Title: Populating Shop Inventory
PopulateShop.Content: Open your newly created Shop Actor. You can populate the shop's inventory by dragging and dropping items from compendiums or the World Items directory directly onto the sheet's inventory or management tab. Alternatively, you can seed items during actor creation using the populate inventory options.

ConnectPlayer.Title: Connect Shop to a Player's Contact
ConnectPlayer.Content: On the Shop Actor Sheet, switch to the Management tab. Toggle the edit mode checkbox in the header to unlock the drop fields. Drag a contact/connection Item from a Player's character sheet onto the Connection drop zone. This links the shop to that player's contact.

LinkEmployee.Title: Link Connection Actor as Employee
LinkEmployee.Content: To allow players to interact with the shop using the contact's skills and network, drag the Actor representing the contact (the Connection Actor) onto the Employees drop zone. You can also select it as the active Serving Employee by clicking the bell icon next to its name.

Placement.Title: Canvas Placement & Shop Size
Placement.Content: Drag the Shop Actor from your directory onto the active scene canvas to place its token. On the Shop's Management tab, configure the Shop Radius in grid units. Controlled player tokens entering this radius will automatically detect the shop!

BuyWithShop.Title: Buying Using a Shop Actor
BuyWithShop.Content: Players can open the shop by double-clicking the shop token on the canvas (when their select tool is active) or by clicking the Shop icon in the MarketShouter bar. They can add items to their shopping basket and submit the request to the GM.

BuyOwn.Title: Buying Using Only Own Actor
BuyOwn.Content: If no shop token is placed or nearby, players can still shop. By opening the MarketShouter bar and searching, they can browse global items and submit requests directly, representing online Matrix shopping or looking for general gear.

GMConfirm.Title: Confirming Requests (GM)
GMConfirm.Content: As a GM, when a player submits a request, you will receive a chat card. Click Review & Confirm on the card, or click the Megaphone icon in the MarketShouter to review the shopping cart, execute availability tests, and approve or reject the items.

Conclusion.Title: You're All Set!
Conclusion.Content: That concludes the Getting Started tour! You are now ready to run dynamic, lore-friendly marketplace transactions in your Shadowrun 5e games.

Factory.SelectorSubtitle: Select a vehicle or drone in range of the factory to modify or repair it.
Factory.NoVehiclesInRadius: No vehicles or drones detected in range.
